message:
fix(analysis): Propagate scoped variables through type inference

Root cause: inferExprWithInferrer only checked scoped variables for
direct .name lookups, but recursive calls used inferExpr which passed
null for type_inferrer, losing access to function-local variables.

This caused functions returning local variables (e.g., `return result`)
to have their return types inferred as .unknown -> *PyObject instead of
the correct type.

Changes:
- Add scoped variable lookup in .name case of inferExprWithInferrer
- Replace ~35 recursive inferExpr calls with inferExprWithInferrer
- Add type_inferrer parameter to builtin_calls.inferBuiltinCall
- Propagate type_inferrer through calls.zig method dispatch

Fixes function return type inference for local variables.
